input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A static title card features white, scratchy serif text reading "Apple Butter made with dehydrated apples" centered on a dark maroon background. [2-8] The video cuts to a still life arrangement on a patterned cloth against a white wall. A large glass jar filled with dried apple slices sits beside a smaller jar containing a dark brown spread. Behind them stands a ceramic rooster figurine decorated with patriotic stars and stripes. Vertical red text runs along the left edge. [8-14] The scene changes to a white ceramic slow cooker on a countertop. Inside the pot, a quantity of dried apple slices rests at the bottom. In the background, a yellow spray can and other kitchen items are visible. The camera remains fixed on the appliance.

[14-16] A white ceramic slow cooker sits on a countertop containing tan dried apple slices, with a yellow spray can and glass jars visible in the background. [16-20] A person's hand reaches into the basin to stir and lift a large clump of the wrinkled fruit pieces. [20-24] Two hands then work to break apart the clump inside the pot before withdrawing, leaving the slices scattered. [24-28] Finally, a clear plastic bag filled with more dried apple slices is brought over the appliance, and the contents are dumped in to add to the existing layer.

[28-44] A pair of hands transfers tan, dried apple slices from a clear plastic bag into a white ceramic slow cooker. Initially, the person shakes and squeezes the crumpled bag to empty remaining fruit onto an existing pile at the basin's bottom. Subsequently, the hands hold a large, dense clump of dried apple slices above the pot. The person manually pulls apart the mass, separating individual wrinkled pieces and dropping them into the slow cooker until the clump is fully broken down and added to the layer of dehydrated fruit inside.

[44-48] A white ceramic slow cooker basin holds several large clumps of tan dried apple slices resting at the bottom. Background items include a yellow spray can and glass jars. A silver metallic object partially enters the top right corner. [48-56] A silver metallic kettle positions above the basin and pours a continuous stream of clear water directly onto the tan dried apple slices. The liquid flows steadily, moistening and saturating the dehydrated fruit pieces as they hydrate. The text "CROCK-POT" is visible on the front of the white appliance. [56-58] The silver metallic kettle stops dispensing liquid as the clear stream thins and disappears completely. The tan dried apple slices remain resting at the bottom, appearing hydrated from the pouring action, while the kettle spout stays positioned over the pot.

[58-60] A silver metallic kettle remains stationary above a white ceramic slow cooker after ceasing flow, with tan dried apple slices sitting hydrated in liquid at the bottom. [60-64] A thin, clear stream resumes pouring from the kettle onto the tan dried apple slices, steadily saturating the fruit pile within the white basin as the "CROCK-POT" logo is visible on the appliance's exterior and a yellow spray can sits in the background. [64-68] Steam rises as the water level increases while the clear stream continues hitting the tan dried apple slices, further hydrating the fruit. [68-70] The stream shifts to fall directly onto the tan dried apple slices on the right side of the pot. [70-72] The silver kettle ceases pouring, leaving the fruit in liquid, and a hand with visible fingers enters the frame on the right near the pot's rim.

recent_thinks: [72-74] The white ceramic slow cooker remains stationary on the countertop, filled with tan dried apple slices submerged in liquid. No water stream is visible pouring from above, and the hand seen previously on the right edge has withdrawn from the frame. The hydrated fruit pieces sit undisturbed at the bottom of the basin.

[74-76] The scene cuts to a close-up of the slow cooker basin filled with plump, light tan cooked apple pieces. A silver metal fork rests among the soft, rehydrated fruit slices. The apples appear fully hydrated and tender, contrasting with the dried state.

[76-78] The static close-up shot continues displaying the white ceramic basin filled with plump, light tan cooked apple pieces. The silver metal fork remains stationary among the soft, rehydrated fruit slices. The apples sit fully hydrated and tender within the container, with the arrangement and lighting conditions remaining constant without any visible motion or interaction.

[78-80] The static close-up shot persists, displaying the white ceramic basin filled with plump, light tan cooked apple pieces. A silver metal fork rests motionless among the soft, rehydrated fruit slices. No visible movement occurs as the arrangement, lighting, and camera angle remain entirely constant throughout this segment.

[80-82] The close-up shot displays a white ceramic basin containing plump, tan cooked apple slices and a silver fork. The scene remains largely static until a blurry finger or hand partially appears on the far left edge, hovering near the rim while the food and utensil remain undisturbed.

queries.question: What pattern is visible on the body of the decorative rooster figurine? A. American flag B. Polka dots C. Plaid D. Solid green

samples.output: <think>A hand reaches in from the left edge, lifting a single piece of the cooked, tan apple slice into the air. The silver fork remains stationary amidst the pile of plump, rehydrated fruit inside the white ceramic basin. The lifted slice hangs vertically, showcasing its soft texture.</think><action>silent</action>
